tool_5x_signature: enable_deterministic_mode(seed: int = 42, physics_dt: float = 1/60, solver_iterations: int = 4)
cite: In Isaac Sim 5.x, reproducible replay is configured via enable_deterministic_mode(seed=..., physics_dt=..., solver_iterations=...), which authors TGS solver + fixed timestep + CPU dynamics on /World/PhysicsScene. The 4.x omni.isaac.core.SimulationContext.set_deterministic method was removed in 5.0 and raises ImportError. PhysX floating-point ordering is hardware-dependent, so bit-identical replay is only guaranteed on the same GPU model + CUDA driver; the CI runner must be pinned. For rosbag-diff regression, follow the 'archive-with-version-pin' protocol: ship the Kit build hash, solver config, and seed alongside the USD stage as CI artifacts.
caveats: GPU dynamics (PhysX tensor view) is NOT fully deterministic even on identical hardware; CPU dynamics required.
Never claim 'fully deterministic' without the hardware-pin caveat.
Cross-machine determinism requires identical GPU model AND identical CUDA driver AND identical Kit build.
references: Isaac Sim 5.x release notes — solver TGS/PGS determinism section

cite: In Isaac Sim 5.x the ROS2 bridge moved from omni.isaac.ros2_bridge.* to isaacsim.ros2.nodes (from 5.0 onward; pre-5.0 5.x builds use isaacsim.ros2.bridge). The 4.x omni.isaac.ros2_bridge paths raise ModuleNotFoundError on 5.x. Use setup_ros2_bridge(profile=...) or configure_ros2_bridge(sensors=[...]) which auto-detect the correct namespace for the installed build. Note: rmw_init fails without AMENT_PREFIX_PATH in Kit's environment — Isaac Sim must be launched from a terminal that has sourced the ROS2 distro setup.bash.
caveats: OmniGraph node types use isaacsim.* namespace (NOT omni.isaac.*); use the composite tools that handle the version split.
AMENT_PREFIX_PATH must be set in Kit's process environment (source /opt/ros/<distro>/setup.bash before launching).
references: Isaac Sim ROS2 Bridge Migration Guide — v4 → v5

cite: In Isaac Sim 5.x, URDF import uses isaacsim.asset.importer.urdf.import_urdf(asset_path, cfg) or the registered import_robot tool. The 4.x omni.isaac.urdf and later omni.importer.urdf module paths were consolidated under isaacsim.asset.importer. URDFParseAndImportFile remains available as an omni.kit.commands entry but silently returns (result=False, prim_path=None) on missing file or parse error — always pair it with HasAuthoredReferences / stage.GetPrimAtPath(dest).IsValid() post-checks (import_robot tool already does this).
caveats: URDFParseAndImportFile success signal is a tuple; empty prim_path means failure even with result=True in some Kit versions.
cfg.convex_decomposition=False (convex hull) is the safe default for collision meshes.

physx_rule_5x: ArticulationRootAPI must be authored on the ROBOT ROOT PRIM (not a parent Xform group)
cite: Isaac Sim 5.x's PhysX tensor API (used by ArticulationView for batched RL) pattern-matches on the prim path where PhysicsArticulationRootAPI is applied. Moving the API to a parent Xform (e.g. /World/envs instead of /World/envs/env_0/Franka) breaks tensor-view registration silently — trajectories read as zeros. The 4.x omni.isaac.core.articulations.Articulation class was replaced by isaacsim.core.prims.SingleArticulation in 5.x. For cloning, use isaacsim.core.cloner.GridCloner which preserves the articulation-root marker on each clone's robot root.
caveats: Never move ArticulationRootAPI off the robot root — ArticulationView.initialize fails silently.
GridCloner is for layout batching, NOT the same as Replicator's DR cloner (different purposes).

cite: In Isaac Sim 5.x, the omni.isaac.core.* namespace was consolidated under isaacsim.core.* — omni.isaac.core.articulations.Articulation became isaacsim.core.prims.SingleArticulation, omni.isaac.core.World became isaacsim.core.api.World, omni.isaac.core.prims.XFormPrim became isaacsim.core.prims.SingleXFormPrim. Importing from omni.isaac.core.* raises ModuleNotFoundError on 5.0+ installs. The migration is mechanical — most class names survive, only the module path changes.
caveats: Some classes renamed (XFormPrim → SingleXFormPrim, Articulation → SingleArticulation) to distinguish single-instance from batched ArticulationView variants.
Always prefer isaacsim.* imports in new code; existing 4.x training scripts must migrate before running on 5.x.

deprecated_4x: rep.randomizer.color() for material inputs (wrong API — use rep.modify.material_property or direct USD)
cite: Replicator (omni.replicator.core) DR works via rep.modify.pose, rep.modify.visibility, rep.modify.semantics, rep.modify.material_property, and rep.randomizer.scatter_2d / scatter_3d. There is NO rep.randomizer.color() — for material color randomization use rep.modify.material_property with the correct MDL input name, or randomize via direct UsdShade.Material mutation. For per-instance material randomization (not uniform-all), each prim needs its own material instance first (the shared-material pitfall).
caveats: Sharing one material across N prims and randomizing the material affects all N uniformly — not per-instance. Duplicate the material first.
rep.trigger.on_frame is needed for randomizers to fire on each Replicator step.

physx_rule_5x: PhysicsArticulationRootAPI MUST be on the robot root prim — moving it to a parent Xform breaks tensor-view registration silently
cite: In Isaac Sim 5.x PhysX tensor views (isaacsim.core.prims.ArticulationView, used by batched RL) pattern-match on the exact prim path where PhysicsArticulationRootAPI is applied. Moving the API to a parent Xform (e.g. /World/envs/env_0 instead of /World/envs/env_0/Franka) causes ArticulationView.initialize to register zero articulations — trajectories read as empty arrays. Always apply ArticulationRootAPI on the robot's actual root prim, not on an ancestor group.
caveats: Error is silent — empty trajectory arrays without exception.
GridCloner preserves this correctly; hand-rolled cloning loops often don't.

tool_5x: create_conveyor_track (full geometry + motion) OR manual PhysxSurfaceVelocityAPI
cite: In Isaac Sim 5.x a moving conveyor belt is implemented via PhysX surface-velocity — the belt mesh stays still, but objects in collision contact inherit a horizontal velocity from the belt's surface. **CRITICAL — DO NOT use the `create_conveyor` tool; it expects a pre-existing prim with specific structure and returns success=false in nearly every real scenario (observed 3 times on 2026-04-19). Apply the three APIs directly via run_usd_script instead.** The belt prim needs THREE APIs applied together: (1) UsdPhysics.CollisionAPI.Apply(belt_prim), (2) UsdPhysics.RigidBodyAPI.Apply(belt_prim) followed by setting kinematicEnabled=True (PhysX only integrates surface-velocity on kinematic rigid bodies; a plain collider is ignored — this is the #1 cause of 'belt is configured but cubes just sit on it'), (3) PhysxSchema.PhysxSurfaceVelocityAPI.Apply(belt_prim), then set 'physxSurfaceVelocity:surfaceVelocity' = Gf.Vec3f(vx,vy,vz), 'physxSurfaceVelocity:surfaceVelocityEnabled' = True, 'physxSurfaceVelocity:surfaceVelocityLocalSpace' = True. Objects that should ride the belt need UsdPhysics.RigidBodyAPI + UsdPhysics.CollisionAPI themselves AND must sit ON the belt top surface (translate.z = belt_top_world_z + object_world_half_height) — a submillimeter gap = no contact = no velocity transfer.
caveats: PhysxSurfaceVelocityAPI requires a kinematic rigid body — a static collider alone does not transfer velocity.
surfaceVelocityLocalSpace=True means vx/vy/vz are in the BELT's local frame; rotate the belt prim and the motion direction rotates with it. Set to False for world-frame velocity.
Objects need PhysicsRigidBodyAPI + PhysicsCollisionAPI to receive surface velocity — kinematic bodies or pure colliders will not move.
A gap between object and belt (even sub-mm) means no contact, no velocity transfer. Sit objects on the belt top: translate_z = belt_top_world_z + (object_world_half_height).
references: Isaac Sim 5.x physics manual — kinematic rigid bodies

cite: In Isaac Sim 5.1 the canonical Franka Panda asset URL is https://omniverse-content-production.s3-us-west-2.amazonaws.com/Assets/Isaac/5.1/Isaac/Robots/FrankaRobotics/FrankaPanda/franka.usd — note the FrankaRobotics/FrankaPanda subdirectory (NOT Isaac/Robots/Franka/franka.usd which 404s, NOT FrankaEmika/panda/panda.usd which 404s, NOT the deprecated 4.2 panda_instanceable.usd with http:// prefix which 404s on 5.x endpoints). **CRITICAL verification after AddReference**: Kit's `AddReference` and the `robot_wizard` / `anchor_robot` tools ALL return success=true even when the asset fails to resolve at composition time. `HasAuthoredReferences()` = True is NOT proof the Franka loaded. Instead, count the imported prim's children: `len(list(prim.GetAllChildren()))` must be ≥ 10 for a real Franka (links, joints). If 0 children → the reference 404'd silently; the asset URL was wrong. Franka reach envelope: sphere r=0.855m from base joint, minus inner column r=0.2m. Default gripper (2-finger parallel) opens 80mm → grasp-targets must be ≤60mm for reliable grip.
caveats: Bare `HasAuthoredReferences()` check passes even on 404 asset URLs — verify children count AFTER reference resolution.
Gripper is 2-finger parallel, opens 80mm. Cubes larger than 60mm won't grip reliably.
Franka reach 0.855m from joint 1 — place pick/drop points within this envelope.
Default Isaac Sim 5.x asset root is read from /persistent/isaac/asset_root/default setting; AWS S3 paths under /5.1/ use https:// prefix not http://.
references: Isaac Sim 5.1 asset catalog — FrankaRobotics subtree

cite: An 'open-top bin' (container for pick-and-place drop-off) is NOT a single solid Cube — that's a filled block objects can't enter. The minimal correct shape is FIVE thin Cube prims under a parent Xform: a floor slab + four vertical walls. Each wall gets UsdPhysics.CollisionAPI so objects colliding with them stop; the bin parent gets no API. Canonical pattern for a 0.3 × 0.3 × 0.15m open bin at (0, 0, 0): parent Xform /World/Bin; /World/Bin/Floor = Cube with scale (0.3, 0.3, 0.01), translate (0, 0, 0); /World/Bin/WallX1 = Cube scale (0.01, 0.3, 0.15), translate (-0.15, 0, 0.075); WallX2 mirrored at (+0.15, 0, 0.075); WallY1/Y2 at (0, ±0.15, 0.075) with scale (0.3, 0.01, 0.15). Apply PhysicsCollisionAPI to each of the 5 child Cubes. The bin parent Xform is where you set the overall world transform; children get identity local transforms EXCEPT for their wall-specific offsets above.
caveats: A single Cube labeled /World/Bin with solid geometry is a common LLM mistake — objects cannot enter a filled block.
Walls need CollisionAPI on the CHILD Cubes, not the parent. Parent gets no physics API.
Wall thickness 0.01m is minimum reliable — thinner causes PhysX contact-detection miss at high object velocity.
For a bin that should stop objects AND be visible: also apply PhysxCollisionAPI with approximation='convexHull' (default) on each wall.
references: Isaac Sim physics manual — collision primitives
